metadata.title: Competitor Virtual Currency Price Changes
metadata.description: Documented price change events for major game virtual currencies (Fortnite V-Bucks, Apex Legends Coins, and others)

price_change_events.mechanism: Reduced V-Bucks granted per dollar spent (prices stayed the same, quantities decreased)
price_change_events.stated_reason: The cost of running Fortnite has gone up a lot and we're raising prices to help pay the bills.

tier_changes.notes: Dollar prices unchanged. V-Bucks quantities reduced at every tier. Effective price per V-Buck increased ~20-25% depending on tier.

battle_pass_changes.notes: Battle Pass cost lowered from 1000 to 800 V-Bucks. Completion now returns exactly 800 V-Bucks (net zero). Previously returned 1000 + 500 bonus = net +500 V-Bucks. Players can no longer profit V-Bucks from the Battle Pass.

price_change_events.mitigation_offered: 20% Epic Rewards credit on purchases made through Epic's own storefront/payment system.
price_change_events.player_reception: Highly negative. Widely seen as the most significant Fortnite monetisation change since launch.
price_change_events.significance: First ever V-Bucks price increase in Fortnite's history (launched 2017). Epic had maintained stable pricing for ~9 years.

price_change_events.mechanism: Direct price changes in affected currencies
price_change_events.stated_reason: Changes in global economic conditions and currency valuations.
currencies_decreased: Some regions saw decreases to rebalance global pricing
price_change_events.significance: USD pricing has never changed for Apex Coins. Only non-USD currencies were adjusted.

additional_change_2024.description: Battle Pass could no longer be purchased with Apex Coins, only with real money. EA stated this allowed them to decrease the Premium+ price.

price_change_events.notes: COD Points pricing has remained stable since introduction. No documented USD price increases. Points carry over between annual COD titles.

industry_trends: USD virtual currency prices have been remarkably stable across the industry. Most publishers avoid direct USD price increases.
When price adjustments occur, they typically affect non-USD currencies first, citing exchange rate fluctuations (EA in June 2023, Apex in June 2023).
Fortnite's March 2026 change was notable as the first major USD-affecting price increase from a top-tier live-service game, using quantity reduction rather than price increases.
The preferred industry strategy is tier restructuring (adding higher-priced tiers) rather than increasing prices on existing tiers.
EA's approach from FIFA 19 to FC 26: raised the spending ceiling from $39.99 to $149.99 without ever increasing the per-point cost at any existing price point.
Battle Pass monetisation is shifting across the industry: both Fortnite and Apex have reduced or eliminated the ability to 'earn back' premium currency through pass completion.
